name: 莫里斯·拉威尔
brief: 拉威尔出生于法国西南部靠近西班牙边境的希布尔，母亲是巴斯克人，这一血统对他的音乐产生了深远影响。他在巴黎音乐学院师从福雷，曾五次角逐罗马大奖均告失败，引发了著名的「拉威尔事件」丑闻。一战期间他执意参军，担任卡车司机。他终身未婚，生活精致而孤独。1932年遭遇车祸后逐渐出现脑部疾病症状，最终不能写作、说话，1937年在脑部手术后去世。
briefEn: Ravel was born in Ciboure, near the Spanish border in southwestern France. His mother was Basque, a heritage that profoundly shaped his music. He studied under Fauré at the Paris Conservatoire and failed to win the Prix de Rome five times — a controversy that became the infamous "Ravel affair." During World War I he insisted on enlisting, serving as a truck driver. He never married and led a life of refined solitude. After a taxi accident in 1932 he gradually developed neurological deterioration, eventually losing the ability to write or speak. He died in 1937 following brain surgery.
contribution: 拉威尔被斯特拉文斯基称为「瑞士钟表匠」——他的管弦乐配器术达到了西方音乐史上最精确、最辉煌的高度。他的《波莱罗》以一个简单旋律的不断重复和持续渐强展示了管弦乐色彩的无穷变化，成为20世纪最受欢迎的管弦乐作品。他将印象主义的音色探索与古典主义的形式精确完美结合，开创了一种独特的新古典印象主义风格。
contributionEn: Stravinsky famously called Ravel "the Swiss watchmaker" — his orchestration reached a degree of precision and brilliance unmatched in Western music. Boléro, built on a single melody in relentless repetition and crescendo, showcases an inexhaustible kaleidoscope of orchestral color and became the most popular orchestral work of the twentieth century. He fused Impressionist timbral exploration with Classical formal rigor, forging a distinctive neo-Classical Impressionist idiom.
style: 拉威尔的音乐以精密如钟表的工艺和华丽绚烂的音色著称。与德彪西的朦胧不同，拉威尔的音乐轮廓清晰、节奏精确、结构明朗。他对西班牙音乐和爵士乐的吸收比任何同时代法国作曲家都更加深入。他的钢琴作品将炫技性与音乐性推向了极致——《加斯巴尔之夜》至今仍是钢琴文献中最难的作品之一。
styleEn: Ravel's music is renowned for its clockwork craftsmanship and dazzling orchestral color. Where Debussy is hazy, Ravel is sharply defined — rhythmically precise, structurally lucid, every contour etched with care. He absorbed Spanish music and jazz more deeply than any French contemporary. His piano writing pushes virtuosity and musicality to their limits — Gaspard de la nuit remains one of the most technically demanding works in the entire piano repertoire.

famousScores.name: 《波莱罗》
famousScores.desc: 单一旋律的不断重复与渐强，管弦乐配器的万花筒
famousScores.descEn: A single melody in ceaseless repetition and crescendo — a kaleidoscope of orchestral color
famousScores.detail:
《波莱罗》（1928）最初是应舞蹈家伊达·鲁宾斯坦之托创作的芭蕾舞曲。拉威尔以西班牙波莱罗舞曲的节奏为基础，构建了一个极端的音乐实验：一段旋律（由两个各16小节的主题交替构成）在小军鼓几乎不变的节拍下，由最弱到最强，用不同的乐器组合反复演奏了18遍，历时约15分钟，最后在全体总奏的轰鸣中，以突如其来的E大调转调结束。

拉威尔本人曾说：「我只写了一件真正优秀的作品，那就是《波莱罗》——但遗憾的是，那里面没有任何音乐。」这句话既是自嘲，也是深刻的自我洞察。1928年11月22日首演于巴黎，女主角伊达·鲁宾斯坦在一张大桌子上翩翩起舞，观众席上一位女士激动地高呼「天才！」——据说拉威尔冷静地回答：「那位女士明白了。」

《波莱罗》是20世纪最常被演奏的管弦乐作品之一，也是管弦乐配器教学中不可或缺的范本——通过18次重复中不断变换的乐器组合，可以完整地「透视」现代管弦乐队的每一种音色。拉威尔将自己描述为「一台没有灵魂的机器」时所追求的那种冷峻完美，在《波莱罗》中得到了最纯粹的体现。
famousScores.detailEn:
Boléro (1928) was originally composed as a ballet for the dancer Ida Rubinstein. Ravel built a radical musical experiment on the rhythm of the Spanish bolero dance: a single melody (alternating between two sixteen-bar themes) is repeated eighteen times under the nearly unchanging beat of a snare drum, growing from the faintest whisper to a colossal roar through ever-changing instrumental combinations, over roughly fifteen minutes, before ending with a sudden modulation to E major in full orchestral blaze.

Ravel himself once said: "I have written only one masterpiece — Boléro. Unfortunately there is no music in it." The remark is self-deprecating but also a profound act of self-knowledge. At the premiere on November 22, 1928, with Ida Rubinstein dancing on a large table, a woman in the audience cried out "Genius!" — Ravel is said to have replied calmly: "That woman understands."

Boléro is one of the most frequently performed orchestral works of the twentieth century, and an indispensable teaching model for orchestration: its eighteen repetitions, each with different instrumental combinations, provide a complete "X-ray" of every timbre in the modern orchestra. The cold perfection that Ravel was pursuing when he described himself as "a machine without a soul" finds its purest expression in Boléro.

famousScores.name: 《达芙妮与克洛伊》
famousScores.desc: 为迪亚基列夫的俄罗斯芭蕾舞团而作，管弦乐的极致华美
famousScores.descEn: Written for Diaghilev's Ballets Russes, an orchestral tour de force of ravishing beauty
famousScores.detail:
《达芙妮与克洛伊》（1909—1912）是拉威尔为迪亚基列夫的俄罗斯芭蕾舞团创作的三幕芭蕾，取材自古希腊田园牧歌式小说，讲述牧羊人达芙妮与克洛伊的纯洁爱情。拉威尔将其定位为「以音乐构成的交响诗」，整部作品加入了无词合唱，使管弦乐队在人声音色的配合下获得了前所未有的饱满。

创作期间，拉威尔与迪亚基列夫、编舞者福金（Michel Fokine）的关系颇为紧张——福金不断要求缩减时间，拉威尔则坚守自己的音乐完整性。首演于1912年6月8日，弗拉基米尔·尼金斯基出演达芙妮，卡萨维娜出演克洛伊，首演效果平平，但随后的演出季中，作品逐渐建立起崇高的声望。

第三幕的黎明场景（「黎明」，Lever du jour）是整部作品最令人叹为观止的段落——管弦乐以极弱的音量从夜色中缓缓浮现，如同大自然在睡梦中苏醒，随后逐渐洗亮，最终在全体总奏中爆发出辉煌的日出。这段约7分钟的音乐常被单独提取演奏，被许多指挥家和音乐学家视为西方管弦乐文献中最美的段落之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Daphnis et Chloé (1909–12) is a three-act ballet written for Diaghilev's Ballets Russes, drawn from an ancient Greek pastoral novel about the innocent love of the shepherd Daphnis and the shepherdess Chloé. Ravel described it as "a choreographic symphony" — the full score incorporates a wordless chorus, giving the orchestra an unprecedented richness of color through the addition of human voices.

The creative process was fraught: Ravel clashed with Diaghilev and choreographer Michel Fokine, who repeatedly demanded cuts while Ravel insisted on musical integrity. The premiere on June 8, 1912, with Nijinsky as Daphnis and Karsavina as Chloé, was received coolly, but the work's reputation grew steadily in subsequent seasons.

The dawn scene of Act III ("Lever du jour") is the most breathtaking passage in the entire score — the orchestra emerges from darkness at the softest possible volume, as if nature stirring in sleep, gradually brightening until it erupts in a blazing, full-orchestral sunrise. This roughly seven-minute passage is frequently extracted for concert performance and is considered by many conductors and musicologists one of the most beautiful passages in the entire Western orchestral literature.

famousScores.name: 《加斯巴尔之夜》
famousScores.desc: 三首钢琴诗，被认为是钢琴文献中技术最难的作品之一
famousScores.descEn: Three piano poems widely regarded as one of the most technically formidable works in the repertoire
famousScores.detail:
《加斯巴尔之夜》（1908）由三首钢琴诗构成，灵感来自阿洛伊修斯·贝特朗（Aloysius Bertrand）1842年同名诗集：《水妖》（Ondine）、《绞刑台》（Le Gibet）和《幻灵》（Scarbo）。拉威尔创作时明确表示希望写一部「比巴拉基列夫的《伊斯拉美》更难的作品」（巴拉基列夫的《伊斯拉美》当时被认为是最难的钢琴曲）。他最终成功了。

第一首《水妖》描绘水中仙女引诱人类的传说，以极快的速度演奏的半音经过句和颤音模拟水的流动，结尾处的长篇颤音段落是技术上极为艰险的考验。第二首《绞刑台》全曲以极弱的音量贯穿始终，在固定的降B上震鸣如教堂钟声——在长达七分钟的时间里，演奏者必须以完全相同的力度演奏这个单音，而不能有分毫的波动，被认为是钢琴文献中最考验心理耐力的段落。第三首《幻灵》以惊人的速度和复杂的双手交叉描绘鬼怪的诡异出没。

《加斸巴尔之夜》是20世纪钢琴文献中最受尊重的作品之一，它的技术挑战与音乐深度相匹配——三首诗分别代表了诱惑、虚无与恐惧三个人类最深层的心理原型，是拉威尔将音乐推向极端时展现出的那种精准与狠辣的最佳体现。
famousScores.detailEn:
Gaspard de la nuit (1908) consists of three piano poems inspired by the 1842 prose-poetry collection of the same name by Aloysius Bertrand: Ondine (the water sprite), Le Gibet (the gallows), and Scarbo (the goblin). Ravel explicitly stated his intention to compose a piece "more difficult than Balakirev's Islamey" — at the time considered the hardest piano work in existence. He succeeded.

The first piece, Ondine, depicts a water nymph luring a human, its rapid chromatic runs and trills simulating flowing water, with a long trill passage near the end that is one of the most technically treacherous passages in the repertoire. Le Gibet maintains an almost inaudible dynamic throughout, with a repeated B-flat tolling like a church bell — for seven minutes the pianist must sustain this single note at an absolutely identical volume without the faintest fluctuation, considered the most demanding test of psychological endurance in all piano literature. The third piece, Scarbo, depicts a goblin's sinister appearances through astonishing speed and complex hand-crossing.

Gaspard de la nuit is one of the most respected works in the twentieth-century piano literature, its technical challenge perfectly matched by its musical depth: the three poems respectively embody seduction, nihilism, and terror — three of the most primal psychological archetypes — representing the precision and ruthlessness Ravel brought to bear when pushing music toward its absolute limits.

famousScores.name: 《G大调钢琴协奏曲》
famousScores.desc: 融合爵士元素，慢乐章是拉威尔最深情的旋律
famousScores.descEn: Infused with jazz, its slow movement contains Ravel's most tenderly lyrical melody
famousScores.detail:
《G大调钢琴协奏曲》（1929—1931）是拉威尔两首钢琴协奏曲中较为轻盈明快的一首，深受他1928年美国巡演期间接触的爵士乐和蓝调的影响。第一乐章以鞭声切入（这在协奏曲开头是前所未有的大胆），随后展开一幅色彩斑斓的「美国—西班牙」混血音乐画卷，长笛独奏充满爵士风情的蓝调音阶令人惊喜。

第二乐章是这部协奏曲的灵魂所在，也是拉威尔所有作品中情感最深沉的段落之一。一段极简洁的钢琴独奏旋律，以不可思议的纯净和安详缓缓展开，弦乐仅在很久之后才轻声加入。拉威尔承认，这个旋律耗费他最多时间——他是一个音符一个音符地锻造出来的，而他的灵感来源正是莫扎特的《A大调单簧管五重奏》慢乐章。

拉威尔原本计划自己首演这部协奏曲，但健康状况每况愈下，最终将钢琴独奏部分交给了年轻的玛格丽特·朗，自己指挥。1932年1月14日在巴黎首演，大获成功。这部协奏曲以其将爵士语汇与古典形式完美融合的方式，成为20世纪最具跨界精神的钢琴协奏曲，至今仍是音乐厅中最常被演奏的协奏曲之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Concerto in G major (1929–31) is the lighter and brighter of Ravel's two piano concertos, strongly influenced by the jazz and blues he encountered during his 1928 American tour. The first movement opens with a whip-crack — an astonishing coup de théâtre at the very start of a concerto — before unfolding a vivid "American-Spanish" hybrid musical panorama, with a solo flute line colored by jazz-inflected blue notes.

The second movement is the soul of the concerto and one of the most emotionally profound passages in all of Ravel's output. An extremely spare piano melody unfolds with incredible purity and serenity; the strings enter quietly only much later. Ravel acknowledged that this melody cost him the most effort of anything he ever wrote — forged note by note, drawing direct inspiration from the slow movement of Mozart's Clarinet Quintet in A major.

Ravel had planned to give the premiere himself, but his deteriorating health forced him to hand the solo part to the young Marguerite Long while he conducted. The Paris premiere of January 14, 1932 was a great success. With its seamless fusion of jazz idiom and classical form, this concerto has become the most cross-genre piano concerto of the twentieth century and remains among the most frequently performed concertos in the concert hall.

famousScores.name: 《鹅妈妈组曲》
famousScores.desc: 原为四手联弹，童话般的纯真与精致
famousScores.descEn: Originally for piano four hands — fairy-tale innocence rendered with exquisite refinement
famousScores.detail:
《鹅妈妈》（1908—1910）最初是拉威尔为他的朋友米慕和让·戈代布斯基（Mimie and Jean Godebski）两个孩子而写的四手联弹组曲，后来被改编为管弦乐组曲（1911），再后来又被扩充为完整的芭蕾舞剧（1912）。这部作品以查尔斯·佩罗和其他法国童话故事为素材，包含《睡美人》《小拇指》《丑八怪与美女》《美丽的鹦鹉国公主》和《仙女园》。

管弦乐版本是这部组曲流传最广的形式，它展现了拉威尔最节制、最纯真的一面。这里没有《波莱罗》的炫耀，没有《加斯巴尔之夜》的技术角斗，只有一种孩子般纯澈的目光和精心调配的音色。五音阶的运用、五音列的旋律、甘美兰式的共鸣，共同营造了一种来自童年梦境的神秘氛围。

拉威尔自己说，他在写这部作品时追求的是「儿童的诗意」（la poésie de l'enfance），而不是描绘给成人看的「关于儿童的诗意」。这一细微但重要的区别，在《仙女园》末尾那段悠然展开的仙境中得到了最充分的体现——那是拉威尔留给世界的最温柔的音乐礼物之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Ma mère l'Oye (1908–10) was originally composed as a piano four-hands suite for the two young children of Ravel's friends Mimie and Jean Godebski, later orchestrated (1911) and finally expanded into a full ballet (1912). It draws on fairy tales by Charles Perrault and other French sources, including Sleeping Beauty, Tom Thumb, Beauty and the Beast, The Enchanted Garden, and Laideronette.

The orchestral version is the most widely performed form of the suite, and it reveals Ravel's most restrained and innocent side. Here there is none of Boléro's showmanship, none of Gaspard's technical combat — only a childlike clarity of vision and exquisitely calibrated tone color. Pentatonic scales, five-note melodies, and gamelan-like resonances together conjure an atmosphere drawn from the dreams of childhood.

Ravel said he was aiming for "the poetry of childhood" (la poésie de l'enfance) — not the poetry "about" childhood written for adult eyes. This subtle but crucial distinction finds its fullest expression in the serenely unfolding enchanted garden at the end of the suite — one of the tenderest musical gifts Ravel ever bestowed on the world.

famousScores.name: 《左手钢琴协奏曲》（D大调）
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Concerto for the Left Hand in D major
famousScores.desc: 为一战中失去右臂的钢琴家维特根斯坦而作，单手演奏的奇迹
famousScores.descEn: Written for Wittgenstein, who lost his right arm in WWI — a miracle of single-handed pianism
famousScores.detail:
《左手钢琴协奏曲》（1929—1930）是拉威尔应奥地利钢琴家保罗·维特根斯坦（哲学家路德维希·维特根斯坦之兄）委托而作。维特根斯坦在第一次世界大战中失去了右臂，拒绝就此放弃钢琴演奏生涯，他委托了多位作曲家为他创作左手独奏协奏曲（包括普罗科菲耶夫和布里顿）。拉威尔的这首是其中最伟大的一首。

作品以单一乐章写成，在约17分钟的篇幅内经历了从缓板的深沉开篇到进行曲的粗犷激昂再到爵士风的灵动轻巧的戏剧性历程。拉威尔的天才之处在于：他让左手的音乐听起来远不止左手——那些宽广的分解和弦、迅疾的音阶和颤音，共同营造出一种饱满的立体感，令人忘记钢琴家只有一只手在演奏。

维特根斯坦收到乐谱后对其大加修改，令拉威尔极度不满——他写信给维特根斯坦说：「演奏者必须演奏作曲家写的东西，而不是他认为应该写的东西。」这段公案成为音乐史上著名的轶事。这部协奏曲因其独特的创作背景和非凡的音乐质量，被认为是拉威尔所有协奏曲中最深沉、最富悲剧性格的作品。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Concerto for the Left Hand (1929–30) was commissioned by the Austrian pianist Paul Wittgenstein (brother of the philosopher Ludwig), who had lost his right arm in the First World War and refused to abandon his career as a performer. Wittgenstein commissioned left-hand concertos from several composers, including Prokofiev and Britten; Ravel's is the greatest of them all.

The work is cast in a single movement that unfolds over roughly seventeen minutes, moving dramatically from a dark, slow opening through a rugged march to a section of jazz-inflected lightness. Ravel's genius lies in making the left hand sound like far more than a single hand — broad arpeggios, swift scales, and trills combine to create a full, three-dimensional texture that makes one forget only one hand is at the keyboard.

On receiving the score, Wittgenstein made extensive modifications to suit his technique, enraging Ravel, who wrote to him: "The performer must play what the composer wrote, not what he thinks the composer should have written." The quarrel became one of the celebrated anecdotes of music history. The concerto, thanks to its unique genesis and extraordinary musical quality, is considered the darkest and most tragic of all Ravel's orchestral works.

famousScores.name: 《F大调弦乐四重奏》
famousScores.desc: 拉威尔唯一的弦乐四重奏，与德彪西的g小调四重奏并称法国双璧
famousScores.descEn: Ravel's only string quartet — the twin pillar of the French chamber music tradition alongside Debussy's
famousScores.detail:
《F大调弦乐四重奏》（1902—1903）是拉威尔在音乐学院学习时的作品，也是他唯一的弦乐四重奏。他的老师福雷对这部作品的结尾并不满意，建议他修改，拉威尔拒绝了——这份初生牛犊不怕虎的坚持，显示出他对自己音乐判断力的高度自信。作品献给德彪西，德彪西听后回信说：「以上帝的名义，不要改动任何一个音符！」

四个乐章中，弦乐拨弦（pizzicato）技法的大量运用是这部四重奏最显著的特色之一——第二乐章以拨弦与弓奏的对比和交织为主要手法，创造出一种轻盈而富于节奏律动的音响效果。拉威尔将德彪西式的印象主义色彩与更为清晰的古典结构感结合，使这部作品在整体风格上比德彪西的g小调四重奏更为明朗和有序。

这部四重奏是拉威尔室内乐创作的核心，与德彪西的g小调弦乐四重奏并称为「法国弦乐四重奏双璧」，在世界各地的音乐会舞台上频繁同场出现。两部作品共同确立了法国弦乐四重奏的传统，并成为20世纪室内乐文献中无可取代的经典。
famousScores.detailEn:
The String Quartet in F major (1902–03) was composed while Ravel was still a student at the Conservatoire; it is his only string quartet. His teacher Fauré was dissatisfied with the finale and urged revision — Ravel refused, demonstrating a confidence in his own musical judgment that would define his career. He dedicated the work to Debussy, who wrote back: "In the name of God, do not change a single note!"

Across the four movements, extensive use of pizzicato is one of the quartet's most distinctive features — the second movement is structured largely around the contrast and interplay of plucked and bowed strings, creating a light, rhythmically animated sound world. Ravel combines Debussian Impressionist color with a clearer classical sense of structure, making the work overall more lucid and orderly than Debussy's G minor Quartet.

This quartet is the cornerstone of Ravel's chamber music output and is paired with Debussy's G minor Quartet as the "twin pillars of French string quartet tradition," frequently appearing together on concert programs worldwide. Together the two works established the French string quartet tradition and have become irreplaceable classics of twentieth-century chamber music.

famousScores.name: 《镜子》（钢琴组曲）
famousScores.desc: 五首钢琴印象画，印象主义钢琴语言的重要里程碑
famousScores.descEn: Five piano impressions — a landmark in the development of Impressionist piano language
famousScores.detail:
《镜子》（1904—1905）是拉威尔五首钢琴小品的组曲，也是他印象主义钢琴风格走向成熟的重要标志。五首作品分别是：《飞蛾》（Noctuelles）、《悲鸟》（Oiseaux tristes）、《海上的扁舟》（Une barque sur l'océan）、《丑角的晨歌》（Alborada del gracioso）和《钟谷》（La vallée des cloches）。这部组曲在和声语言和音响效果上远超他此前的作品，令他的导师福雷和同学们都感到震惊。

其中《悲鸟》是拉威尔本人最钟爱的一首，他说这首曲子「描绘了迷途于炎炎夏日中的鸟儿独自哀鸣的形象」。乐曲几乎没有节奏推进，以孤立的和弦和音程在寂静中漂浮，营造出一种比德彪西更加干燥、更加孤独的音响质感。《丑角的晨歌》则是充满西班牙风情的热烈小品，后来被拉威尔亲自改编为管弦乐版，成为音乐会舞台的热门曲目。

《镜子》的标题来自拉威尔加入的一个象征主义艺术团体「阿帕奇」（Les Apaches）的精神，他们认为艺术是一种反映（映照）内心世界的镜子。这部作品以其对印象主义技法的大胆运用，在法国钢琴文献中占据了独特的历史位置，也是了解拉威尔从学院走向成熟的最重要窗口之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Miroirs (1904–05) is a suite of five piano pieces and a major landmark in the maturation of Ravel's Impressionist piano style. The five works are: Noctuelles (Moths), Oiseaux tristes (Sad Birds), Une barque sur l'océan (A Boat on the Ocean), Alborada del gracioso (The Jester's Morning Song), and La vallée des cloches (The Valley of the Bells). The suite's harmonic language and sonic effects far surpassed anything Ravel had previously written, astonishing his teacher Fauré and his fellow students.

Of the five, Ravel cherished Oiseaux tristes the most, describing it as "the image of birds lost in the torpor of a dark forest during the hottest hours of summer." The piece has almost no rhythmic momentum — isolated chords and intervals float in silence, creating a sonic quality drier and lonelier than anything in Debussy. Alborada del gracioso, by contrast, is a passionate Spanish-flavored miniature that Ravel later orchestrated himself, making it a favorite concert item.

The title Miroirs derives from the spirit of the Symbolist arts circle "Les Apaches" that Ravel had joined, who believed art was a mirror reflecting the inner world. Through its bold deployment of Impressionist technique, this suite holds a unique historical position in the French piano literature and remains one of the most important windows into Ravel's evolution from student to mature master.

famousScores.name: 《圆舞曲》 La Valse
famousScores.desc: 维也纳圆舞曲的末日狂欢，一战的音乐寓言
famousScores.descEn: A Viennese waltz at the end of the world — a musical allegory of the First World War
famousScores.detail:
《圆舞曲》（1919—1920）是拉威尔创作的一首管弦乐「编舞诗」，以维也纳圆舞曲（尤其是施特劳斯圆舞曲传统）为素材，描绘了一个在旋转的舞池中走向毁灭的世界。拉威尔最初在1906年就有了这个创作构思，但直到一战结束后才完成，那场战争的阴影使这部作品获得了一种深刻的历史寓言色彩。

作品从极弱的低音管乐器中浮现，仿佛从远方传来的舞曲声音，渐渐变得清晰；圆舞曲旋律若隐若现，欢快而略带诡异；随后音乐不断地加速、加强，旋律被越来越疯狂的节奏和和声扭曲变形，直至最后在一片混乱的总奏中崩溃——这是一个时代的终结，也是拉威尔对幸福过去的最后告别。

当拉威尔将这部作品提交给迪亚基列夫时，迪亚基列夫拒绝接受，说「这不是芭蕾，而是芭蕾的画像」——这句话令两人的友谊就此决裂，再未修好。后来拉威尔将其改编为双钢琴版本，并亲自演奏。《圆舞曲》如今被认为是20世纪管弦乐文献中最令人惊心动魄的作品之一，其对于「美好时代」（Belle Époque）终结的哀挽，随着时代的推移愈发深入人心。
famousScores.detailEn:
La Valse (1919–20) is a "choreographic poem" for orchestra, drawing on the Viennese waltz tradition — especially Strauss — to depict a world spinning toward destruction on the dance floor. Ravel had conceived the idea as early as 1906, but only completed it after the First World War ended; the shadow of that catastrophe gave the finished work the quality of a profound historical allegory.

The piece emerges from an extremely soft bass-instrument murk, as if a distant dance were gradually becoming audible; waltz melodies flicker in and out, festive yet slightly uncanny; then the music accelerates and intensifies without pause, the melodies increasingly distorted by manic rhythms and dissonant harmonies, until the whole structure collapses in a chaotic final tutti — the end of an era, Ravel's last farewell to a lost happiness.

When Ravel presented the score to Diaghilev, the impresario refused it, saying "This is not a ballet — it is the portrait of a ballet" — a rejection that ended their friendship permanently. Ravel later made a two-piano arrangement and performed it himself. La Valse is now considered one of the most shattering works in the twentieth-century orchestral literature; its elegy for the end of the Belle Époque grows more resonant with every passing decade.

famousScores.name: 《茨冈》（小提琴与钢琴）
famousScores.desc: 向匈牙利吉普赛小提琴传统致敬的狂想曲，技巧炫目至极
famousScores.descEn: A dazzling rhapsody in homage to Hungarian Gypsy violin tradition
famousScores.detail:
《茨冈》（1924）是拉威尔为匈牙利小提琴家叶利·达兰伊（Jelly d'Arányi，曾为巴托克演奏很多作品的名家）而作的一首小提琴狂想曲。「茨冈」在多种中东欧语言中意为「吉普赛人」。拉威尔曾在一次晚宴上听到达兰伊即兴演奏匈牙利吉普赛音乐，被深深迷住，当场决定为她写一首炫技狂想曲。

作品以小提琴无伴奏的长篇独奏开头，模仿了匈牙利吉普赛小提琴家（verbunkos）即兴演奏的传统——各种极端的技法被一一展示：空弦震鸣、双音和弦、高把位的超高音、左手拨弦（左手拨弦同时右手继续弓奏），如同一场小提琴技法的全景展示。钢琴加入后，音乐以越来越快的速度奔向高潮，最终在急速的齐奏中结束。

拉威尔在创作这首作品时，有意借鉴了李斯特的「匈牙利狂想曲」传统和勃拉姆斯处理吉普赛音乐风格的方式，但他的「茨冈」远比两者更加极端——技术难度空前，戏剧性更为狂暴。《茨冈》问世百年来，始终是小提琴家最喜爱的音乐会炫技曲目之一，以其令人窒息的技术挑战和充满野性的音乐热情，在音乐会上无一例外地引发满堂彩。
famousScores.detailEn:
Tzigane (1924) was written for the Hungarian violinist Jelly d'Arányi (a prominent figure who had also premiered many works by Bartók). "Tzigane" means "Gypsy" in several Central and Eastern European languages. Ravel heard d'Arányi improvise Hungarian Gypsy music at a dinner party and was so captivated that he decided on the spot to write a virtuoso rhapsody for her.

The work opens with an extended unaccompanied violin cadenza, imitating the improvising tradition of the Hungarian Gypsy fiddler (verbunkos player) — every extreme technique is displayed in turn: open-string drones, double-stops, stratospheric upper-register passages, left-hand pizzicato (plucking with the left hand while continuing to bow with the right) — a complete panorama of violin technique. Once the piano enters, the music rushes toward a climax at ever-increasing speed, ending in a headlong final unison.

Ravel consciously drew on the Lisztian Hungarian Rhapsody tradition and Brahms's handling of Gypsy-inflected style, but his Tzigane is more extreme than either — technically more demanding, dramatically more savage. Over a century since its premiere, Tzigane has remained one of the most beloved showpieces in the violinist's concert repertoire, its breathtaking technical challenge and untamed musical passion invariably bringing audiences to their feet.

influence: 拉威尔的管弦乐配器技法影响了整个20世纪——从好莱坞电影配乐到爵士大乐队编曲都能听到他的影响。梅西安将拉威尔视为最重要的法国前辈；武满彻的管弦乐音色直接源自拉威尔；约翰·亚当斯的简约主义管弦乐与拉威尔的《波莱罗》有着精神上的关联。他的《G大调钢琴协奏曲》中对爵士元素的运用，预示了古典与流行音乐融合的趋势。
influenceEn: Ravel's orchestration techniques shaped the entire twentieth century — his influence can be heard in everything from Hollywood film scores to jazz big-band arranging. Messiaen regarded him as the most important French predecessor; Tōru Takemitsu's orchestral palette descends directly from Ravel; and John Adams's minimalist orchestral writing shares a spiritual kinship with Boléro. His use of jazz elements in the Piano Concerto in G major anticipated the broader trend of classical-popular fusion.
